title: Cephalocroton zeylanicus
taxonomy: Cephalocroton zeylanicus belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The genus Cephalocroton is a part of the Euphorbiaceae family, which includes over 10,000 species worldwide. This plant is native to Sri Lanka, where it grows in a variety of habitats, from tropical forests to coastal areas. The Euphorbiaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Crotonoideae subfamily to which Cephalocroton zeylanicus belongs. Classification of plants is constantly evolving as new research and discoveries are made, but Cephalocroton zeylanicus is generally considered to be a member of the Euphorbiaceae family.
uses: Cephalocroton zeylanicus is a plant with various traditional medicinal uses, particularly in South Asian countries. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help treat wounds and skin irritations. The leaves and roots of the plant are often used to prepare remedies for fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. In traditional medicine, Cephalocroton zeylanicus is also used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.
